---
title : "Polynomial regression - bias/variance trade-offs for ridge regression"
author : "Thimoté Dupuch"
date : today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    theme: zephyr
engine: julia
---

```{julia}
#| echo : false
#| output : false
using Pkg
Pkg.activate("..")
```


```{julia}
#| output : false
using LinearAlgebra, Random, Statistics, CairoMakie
```


```{julia}
#| output : false
#| echo : false
CairoMakie.set_theme!(theme_latexfonts())
CairoMakie.activate!(type = "svg")
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
colors = Makie.wong_colors()
blue, orange, green, red = colors[1], colors[2], colors[3], colors[6]
```


```{julia}
#| output : false
function generate_grid(std_noise)
    Xgrid = collect(-1:0.001:1)
    Ygrid = Xgrid .^ 2 .- 0.5
    Ygrid_noise = Ygrid .+ randn(length(Xgrid)) .* std_noise
    return Xgrid, Ygrid, Ygrid_noise
end
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
function generate_sample(n, std_noise)
    X = rand(n) .* 2 .- 1
    Y = X .^ 2 .- 0.5 .+ randn(n) .* std_noise
    return X, Y
end
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
function design_matrix(x, kmax)
    n = length(x)
    X = ones(n, kmax + 1)
    for i in 1:kmax
        X[:, i+1] .= x .^ i
    end
    return X
end
```
    
```{julia}
#| output : false
function ridge_solution(X, y, λ)
    n = size(X, 1)
    d = size(X, 2)
    return (X' * X + n * λ * I(d)) \ (X' * y)
end
```



```{julia}
#| output : false
function compute_excess_error(Xgrid, Ygrid, n, lambdas, kmax, std_noise, nrep)
    errors = zeros(nrep, length(lambdas))

    Xgriddata = design_matrix(Xgrid, kmax)

    for irep in 1:nrep
        Xsample, Ysample = generate_sample(n, std_noise)
        Xdata = design_matrix(Xsample, kmax)

        for (j, λ) in enumerate(lambdas)
            w = ridge_solution(Xdata, Ysample, λ)
            Ypred = Xgriddata * w
            errors[irep, j] = mean((Ypred .- Ygrid).^2)
        end
    end

    return errors
end
```


```{julia}
#| output : false
function compute_bias_variance(Xdata, Xgriddata, Ygrid, lambdas, std_noise, n)
    Σ = (Xdata' * Xdata) / n
    d = size(Xdata, 2)

    # optimal weights (no regularization)
    wast = (Xgriddata' * Xgriddata) \ (Xgriddata' * Ygrid)

    bias = zeros(length(lambdas))
    variance = zeros(length(lambdas))

    for (i, λ) in enumerate(lambdas)
        A = inv(Σ + λ * I(d))

        bias[i] = λ^2 * wast' * (Σ * A^2) * wast
        variance[i] = std_noise^2 / n * tr(Σ^2 * A^2)
    end

    return bias, variance
end
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
function plot()
    Random.seed!(1)

    std_noise = 1.0
    n = 300
    nrep = 32
    kmax = 5

    lambdas = 10 .^ (-6:0.25:2)

    # Grid
    Xgrid, Ygrid, _ = generate_grid(std_noise)
    Xgriddata = design_matrix(Xgrid, kmax)

    # Monte Carlo error
    errors = compute_excess_error(Xgrid, Ygrid, n, lambdas, kmax, std_noise, nrep)

    # One sample for bias/variance estimation
    Xsample, Ysample = generate_sample(n, std_noise)
    Xdata = design_matrix(Xsample, kmax)

    bias, variance = compute_bias_variance(
        Xdata, Xgriddata, Ygrid, lambdas, std_noise, n
    )

    fig = Figure(size=(500,400))
    ax = Axis(fig[1, 1],
        title = "Polynomial regression (dim = 5) - Ridge",
        xlabel = L"\log_{10}(\lambda)",
        ylabel = L"\log_{10}(\text{excess risk})",
        xticks = -6:2:2,
        yticks = -3.5:0.5:-1,
        limits = (-6, 2, -3.5, -0.5)
    )

    xvals = log10.(lambdas)
    mean_err = mean(errors, dims=1)[:]
    std_err = std(errors, dims=1)[:]

    # error bars
    errorbars!(
        ax, xvals,
        log10.(mean_err),
        log10.(mean_err .+ std_err) .- log10.(mean_err),
        whiskerwidth = 10,
        label = "Estimated excess risk"
    )

    # curves
    lines!(ax, xvals, log10.(bias), label = "Theoretical bias", color=green)
    lines!(ax, xvals, log10.(variance), label = "Theoretical variance", color=:black)
    lines!(ax, xvals, log10.(bias .+ variance), label = "Bias + Variance", color=red)

    axislegend(ax, position=:lb)
    fig
end
```


```{julia}
plot()
```
